title: Linociera zollingeriana
intro: Linociera zollingeriana, also known as the Zollinger's linociera, is a species of plant in the family Sapindaceae, native to Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, and research has identified various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as fever, cough, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Linociera zollingeriana include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used to make herbal teas, tinctures, and capsules to harness its health benefits.
